description: Figure 4 presents an isothermal section of the Fe-Mn-Al-C system at 850°C with a fixed aluminum content of 8 wt.-%, illustrating the phase fields as functions of manganese (0–25 wt.-%) and carbon (0–1.0 wt.-%) concentrations. The diagram delineates distinct phase regions including single-phase bcc (ferrite), single-phase fcc (austenite), and the intermetallic κ-phase, along with their two-phase and three-phase mixtures (bcc+fcc, bcc+κ, fcc+κ, and bcc+fcc+κ). At low Mn and C contents, the bcc phase dominates, dissolving small amounts of carbon. Increasing carbon promotes κ-phase formation, requiring higher Mn contents to suppress it during continuous annealing. To achieve stable austenite (fcc) at this temperature, both high carbon and high manganese contents are necessary. This phase diagram provides critical thermodynamic guidance for designing three families of lightweight steels—ferritic, duplex (bcc+fcc), and austenitic—while avoiding undesirable κ-phase precipitation in high-Al automotive steel grades.
